goal: wave18-platform-depth: Ship SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 18 (member-independent platform depth) per SITE_DESIGN_v2.md Phase 4 deferrals. Three pillars — no Stripe/credits, no newsletter archive: (1) LLM-assisted ranking — optional experimental re-rank layer on top of existing rule-based engines when MINIMAX_API_KEY (or CHAT_LLM_*) is configured; graceful fallback to deterministic scores when absent or on LLM error. Backend: extend community matcher POST /api/v1/community/match (and session /api/community/match parity) with optional useLlmRerank flag — take rule-based top candidates, LLM re-order + human-readable reason refresh; add POST /api/v1/personal/rank-suggestions for .io library + .org knowledge/community candidates (accept site, candidate summaries, profile context; return ranked ids + reasons). Reuse chat llm.ts patterns. UI: label Experimental LLM assist; show when rerank applied. Tests mock LLM; prove fallback without key. (2) External agent deep links — shared helper module (new packages/shared/src/wave18-external-agent.ts, avoid editing packages/shared/src/index.ts barrel unless required): build ChatGPT + Claude compose URLs from {title, canonicalUrl, suggestedPrompt, site}. Surface as secondary discreet actions alongside Open in Ask on .io library/article + insights cards and .org knowledge/community detail (not hero CTAs). Add brief /for-agents section documenting deep-link pattern + example URLs. Optional JSON agent hint on detail pages (data attribute or script type application/json) with page url + suggested prompts. (3) Phase 2 intent verb UI parity on .org — backend POST /api/community/actions already supports request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply; wire API client + UI so Phase 2 verbs are real persisted actions (with optional short body modal), not Ask-prefill-only. Extend useCommunityInteractions or dedicated hook; detail page ActiveActions shows API buttons for offer_help, request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply per getCommunityActions() from wave13-community taxonomy; keep Ask draft links as secondary where appropriate. Community list/highlights cards: use getCommunityActions not stale COMMUNITY_TYPE_VERBS Phase-1-only map; show type labels + primary verb affordance for Phase 2 types (question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity). Reply affordance for question type. (4) Docs: update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md, docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md, SITE_DESIGN cross-ref if needed. Run pnpm turbo build and pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test. Open ONE draft PR to main. English UI.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 18 platform depth — optional experimental LLM re-rank layer over the rule-based community matcher + a new /api/v1/personal/rank-suggestions (graceful fallback without a key), discreet ChatGPT/Claude external-agent deep links + a /for-agents section on .io and .org, and real persisted Phase 2 community intent verbs (request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply, offer_help) wired through getCommunityActions on .org list + detail
acceptanceCriteria: LLM-assisted ranking is an OPTIONAL re-rank layer on top of the existing rule-based engines, gated on isChatLlmConfigured() (MINIMAX_API_KEY or CHAT_LLM_*): the community matcher POST /api/v1/community/match (and session /api/community/match parity, same router) accepts an optional useLlmRerank flag that, when true AND a key is configured, takes the rule-based top candidates and asks the LLM to re-order them + refresh human-readable reasons; the response indicates whether re-rank was applied (e.g. llmAssisted/rerank.applied) so the UI can label it Experimental. With NO key, an LLM error, or malformed LLM output, it falls back to the deterministic rule-based order/scores and reports llmAssisted=false. Reuses the chat/llm.ts config + fetch patterns (resolveLlmConfig / same /chat/completions call), not a new client.

A new POST /api/v1/personal/rank-suggestions (and session /api/personal/rank-suggestions parity) accepts { site, candidates[] (id + title + summary/kind), profile/context, optional useLlmRerank } for .io library + .org knowledge/community candidates and returns ranked ids + human-readable reasons + an llmAssisted flag; it degrades gracefully to the input/deterministic order with rule-based-style reasons when no key is configured or the LLM errors.

packages/shared/src/wave18-external-agent.ts exports pure helpers that build ChatGPT + Claude compose/deep-link URLs from { title, canonicalUrl, suggestedPrompt, site } (re-exported from the shared barrel so consumers import via @ai-transformation/shared). The shared API client (createApiClient in packages/shared/src/index.ts) gains a community.actions() method (POST /community/actions with { site, objectId, kind, body? }, session + /api/v1 Bearer parity) and a personal.rankSuggestions() method — additive only, without altering the existing agent-entry text the backend agent-protocol test asserts on.

External-agent deep links appear as SECONDARY, discreet actions (not hero CTAs) alongside the existing Open in Ask actions on .io library cards + article/detail and insights cards, and on .org knowledge + community detail. A brief /for-agents section on .io and .org documents the deep-link pattern with example URLs. Optionally, detail pages embed a machine-readable agent hint (data attribute or <script type="application/json">) carrying the page url + suggested prompts.

Phase 2 community intent verbs are REAL persisted actions on .org, not Ask-prefill-only: the community detail ActiveActions renders API-backed buttons for offer_help, request_mentor, ask_for_intro, and apply (with an optional short free-text body modal) driven by getCommunityActions() from the wave13-community taxonomy and posting to /community/actions via the new client method; Ask draft links remain as secondary affordances where appropriate.

Community list/highlights cards on .org use getCommunityActions() (NOT the stale Phase-1-only COMMUNITY_TYPE_VERBS map) to show type labels + a primary verb affordance for Phase 2 types (question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity), plus a reply affordance for the question type.
UI labels the LLM assist as Experimental and only shows the assisted state when re-rank was actually applied; English-only UI; light default + dark toggle preserved.

`pnpm turbo build` passes for ALL build targets (web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes — existing suite stays green and new tests prove the LLM-rerank fallback works WITHOUT a key and (with a mocked LLM) the re-rank path applies.
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 18 shipped; SITE_DESIGN_v2 cross-ref updated if needed.
Exactly ONE draft PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready).

tasks.name: backend-wave18
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 18 BACKEND + SHARED slice ONLY (apps/backend/** and packages/shared/**). Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, or the docs status files — the web UI and docs are separate downstream workers that branch from YOUR branch.

DELIVERABLES:

(1) LLM RE-RANK FOR THE COMMUNITY MATCHER. In apps/backend/src/lanes/community/index.ts the handler `communityRouter.post('/community/match', ...)` is mounted at BOTH /api and /api/v1 (session + Bearer parity). Add an OPTIONAL boolean `useLlmRerank` to the request. Update the request schema `matchExperimentRequestSchema` in packages/shared/src/wave14-community.ts to accept `useLlmRerank?: boolean` (default false). Behaviour: compute the rule-based candidates EXACTLY as today (scoreMatchCandidate → sort → slice(limit)). THEN, only when `useLlmRerank === true` AND `isChatLlmConfigured()` (from apps/backend/src/lanes/chat/llm.ts) is true, ask the LLM to re-ORDER the already-selected top candidates and refresh each candidate's human-readable `reasons`. Implement the re-rank in a NEW module apps/backend/src/lanes/community/llm-rerank.ts that REUSES the chat/llm.ts patterns — import resolveLlmConfig() and call the SAME POST `{baseUrl}/chat/completions` shape (do not invent a second LLM client); prompt the model to return STRICT JSON (e.g. an array of {objectId, reasons[]} or an ordered id list + reasons map); parse defensively. The candidate SET must not change (no new ids, no dropped ids) — the LLM only reorders + rewrites reasons. Extend the response schema `matchExperimentResponseSchema` (wave14-community.ts) with a re-rank indicator, e.g. `llmAssisted: boolean` (and optionally `rerankModel?: string`), so the UI can show an Experimental badge. GRACEFUL FALLBACK (critical): if no key is configured, OR the fetch fails / is non-200, OR the JSON is malformed, OR the returned ids don't match the candidate set, RETURN the deterministic rule-based order/scores with `llmAssisted: false` — never throw, never 500 because of the LLM. Keep the existing /community/match/feedback handler working.

(2) NEW PERSONAL RANK-SUGGESTIONS ENDPOINT. Add `POST /personal/rank-suggestions` mounted at BOTH /api and /api/v1 (session + Bearer parity), following the existing resolveRequester/auth pattern used in apps/backend/src/lanes/objects/index.ts. Prefer a small dedicated lane apps/backend/src/lanes/personal/index.ts exporting a Hono router and mounting it in apps/backend/src/index.ts next to the other `app.route('/api', ...)` / `app.route('/api/v1', ...)` lines (or, if cleaner, add the route to the existing objects lane — your call, but keep auth + mounting consistent). Request: `{ site: 'io'|'org', candidates: Array<{ id: string, title: string, summary?: string, kind?: string }>, context?: { followedTopics?: string[], profileSummary?: string, recentInterests?: string[] } | Record<string, unknown>, useLlmRerank?: boolean, limit?: number }`. Response: `{ experimental: true, site, generatedAt, llmAssisted: boolean, ranked: Array<{ id: string, reason: string }> }`. When `useLlmRerank===true` AND isChatLlmConfigured(), ask the LLM (same /chat/completions reuse, factor shared rerank helper logic so both endpoints share it) to rank the candidate ids and produce one short human-readable reason each; constrain output ids to the input set. FALLBACK when no key / error / malformed: return the candidates in their input order (or a trivial deterministic heuristic) each with a generic rule-based reason and llmAssisted=false. Add request/response zod schemas to packages/shared (e.g. a new packages/shared/src/wave18-ranking.ts, or extend an existing wave file) and re-export from the barrel.

(3) SHARED HELPERS. (a) Create packages/shared/src/wave18-external-agent.ts exporting PURE functions that build ChatGPT + Claude compose/deep-link URLs from `{ title: string, canonicalUrl: string, suggestedPrompt: string, site: 'io'|'org' }` — e.g. buildChatGptDeepLink(input) using https://chatgpt.com/?q=<encoded prompt incl. url> and buildClaudeDeepLink(input) using https://claude.ai/new?q=<encoded prompt incl. url>, plus a buildExternalAgentLinks(input) returning a small typed array [{ provider:'chatgpt'|'claude', label, url }] and a helper to build the default suggested prompt from title+url. URL-encode correctly; keep them dependency-free and unit-testable. Add `export * from './wave18-external-agent'` to packages/shared/src/index.ts. (b) Add `export * from './wave18-ranking'` (if you create that file) to the barrel too. (c) In createApiClient (packages/shared/src/index.ts) add a `community.actions(input: { site, objectId, kind, body? })` method that POSTs to `/community/actions` (and mirror it under the existing `v1` namespace for Bearer), and a `personal.rankSuggestions(input)` method hitting /personal/rank-suggestions (session + v1). ADDITIVE ONLY: do not rename or change existing client methods, and do NOT alter the existing agent-entry / buildAgentQuickStart / 'Harvest Hub' strings in packages/shared/src/index.ts — a backend agent-protocol test asserts on that text.

(4) TESTS (apps/backend, vitest — `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`). The existing tests clear MINIMAX_API_KEY/CHAT_LLM_API_KEY to assert no-key fallback (see apps/backend/src/lanes/chat/chat.test.ts and community/community.test.ts using app.request(...)). Add tests that: (a) WITHOUT a key, POST /api/community/match with useLlmRerank:true returns the same deterministic candidate set/order with llmAssisted:false (prove fallback); (b) WITH the LLM MOCKED (stub global fetch for the /chat/completions call to return a valid JSON re-ordering, after setting a fake key) the response is re-ordered with refreshed reasons and llmAssisted:true, and the candidate id SET is unchanged; (c) a malformed/erroring mocked LLM response still falls back to deterministic order with llmAssisted:false (no 500); (d) POST /api/v1/personal/rank-suggestions without a key returns ranked ids (input set preserved) with llmAssisted:false, and with a mocked LLM returns LLM ordering + reasons with llmAssisted:true. Keep the existing suite green.

Run `pnpm install` first (node_modules are usually missing on cloud start), then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared build` (if it has a build) must pass. Commit to your branch and push. Do NOT open a PR. Your handoff MUST list: the exact endpoint paths + new request/response fields, the new files, the shared client methods added, the deep-link URL formats, and the build+test results (incl. the new fallback + mocked-LLM tests).
tasks.brief:
READ FIRST: docs/waves/wave18-platform-depth.md (scope + DoD) and docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §9–§11 Phase 4 deferrals + §6 contextual actions. Load .agents/skills/lane-backend-core/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/lane-shared/SKILL.md.

KEY EXISTING CODE (reuse, do not reinvent):
- apps/backend/src/lanes/chat/llm.ts exports resolveLlmConfig(): LlmConfig, isChatLlmConfigured(): boolean, generateChatReply(), extractAssistantContent(). Env priority: MINIMAX_API_KEY → CHAT_LLM_API_KEY → OPENAI_API_KEY; base CHAT_LLM_BASE_URL/OPENAI_BASE_URL (MiniMax default https://api.minimax.io/v1 else https://hnd1.aihub.zeabur.ai/v1); model CHAT_LLM_MODEL/MINIMAX_MODEL default MiniMax-M3. It returns null (never throws) when no key or on error — copy that no-throw discipline for your rerank helper. The call is POST {baseUrl}/chat/completions with { model, messages, temperature, max_tokens }.
- apps/backend/src/lanes/community/index.ts: communityRouter.post('/community/match') (~L720) + '/community/match/feedback' (~L782); scoreMatchCandidate(source, candidate), listVisibleCommunityObjects, matcherCompatibility, recordMatchRun(). Mounted in apps/backend/src/index.ts via app.route('/api', communityRouter) and app.route('/api/v1', communityRouter). It also already has communityRouter.post('/community/actions') (~L586) supporting kinds offer_help, request_mentor, ask_for_intro, apply, collaborate — you only need to add the SHARED CLIENT wrapper for it (the backend route is done).
- Schemas in packages/shared/src/wave14-community.ts: matchExperimentRequestSchema { site, objectId, type?, limit? }, matchExperimentResponseSchema { experimental, objectId, type, generatedAt, candidates[], note? }, matchCandidateSchema { objectId, type, title, score, reasons[] }. Extend these (add useLlmRerank to request, llmAssisted to response).
- packages/shared/src/index.ts holds createApiClient(baseUrl) with namespaces incl. community { listByType, getWithReplies, reply, follow, unfollow, offerHelp, join, leave, listInteractions, match, matchFeedback, matchStub } and personalization, and a v1 mirror (Bearer). Add community.actions() + personal.rankSuggestions() (+ v1 mirrors). The barrel uses `export * from './waveNN-...'`; wave17-newsletter.ts exists but is intentionally NOT re-exported — follow the existing per-file re-export style for your new wave18 files.
- Backend tests are integration-style (apps/backend/vitest.config.ts include src/**/*.test.ts): mkdtemp SQLITE_PATH, vi.resetModules(), dynamic import('../../index.js'), app.request('http://localhost/api/...'). For mocking the LLM, set a fake key env then vi.spyOn(globalThis,'fetch') (delegating non-LLM URLs to the real fetch, or just intercept the /chat/completions URL) — or refactor the rerank helper to accept an injected fetch for testability. Prove fallback by deleting MINIMAX_API_KEY/CHAT_LLM_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY.

Backend + combined run via tsx (packages/shared exports .ts); backend tsc excludes *.test.ts. Keep backend host-agnostic. English code. Do NOT touch apps/web-* or apps/combined; the web workers branch from YOUR pushed branch, so your endpoint shapes + client methods + the wave18-external-agent helper are their contract — make the handoff explicit about exact names and signatures.
acceptance: POST /api/community/match + /api/v1/community/match accept optional useLlmRerank; with a key+mocked LLM they reorder the SAME candidate set with refreshed reasons and llmAssisted:true; without a key (or on LLM error/malformed output) they return the deterministic rule-based order with llmAssisted:false and never 500. matchExperimentRequestSchema/matchExperimentResponseSchema updated accordingly.

POST /api/personal/rank-suggestions + /api/v1/personal/rank-suggestions exist with the documented request/response shape, gated LLM assist, and graceful no-key/error fallback (input set preserved, llmAssisted:false).
Re-rank reuses chat/llm.ts config + /chat/completions call (apps/backend/src/lanes/community/llm-rerank.ts or a shared backend helper); no second bespoke LLM client; no-throw discipline.

packages/shared/src/wave18-external-agent.ts exports dependency-free ChatGPT + Claude deep-link builders from { title, canonicalUrl, suggestedPrompt, site }; re-exported from the barrel. createApiClient gains community.actions() + personal.rankSuggestions() (session + v1), additive only, without changing the agent-entry/'Harvest Hub' text the agent-protocol test asserts on.

New backend tests prove: no-key fallback for match + rank-suggestions (llmAssisted:false, set preserved), mocked-LLM rerank applies (llmAssisted:true, set unchanged), and malformed/erroring LLM still falls back without 500. `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` + `test` pass with no regressions; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared` typechecks/builds. No files under apps/web-* or apps/combined changed.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` if node_modules missing.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (existing suite + new LLM-rerank/rank-suggestions tests) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` must pass; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared build`/typecheck passes.
- Assert: match with useLlmRerank:true and NO key → identical candidate ids/order, llmAssisted:false; with a fake key + mocked /chat/completions returning a valid reordering → reordered + refreshed reasons, llmAssisted:true, same id set; malformed mocked LLM → deterministic fallback, no 500.
## Manual / code inspection
- Confirm /personal/rank-suggestions mounted at /api and /api/v1; community.actions() + personal.rankSuggestions() exist in createApiClient; wave18-external-agent.ts re-exported from the barrel; schemas updated in wave14-community.ts (+ any wave18 schema file).
## Gotchas
- LLM helper must never throw/500 the request. Reuse chat/llm.ts patterns. Do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts agent-entry/'Harvest Hub' text. English only. Do not touch apps/web-* or apps/combined.
